facts.text: Norfolk was incorporated in 1758, originally named after Norfolk, England, and locals sometimes pronounce it differently.
Norfolk’s historic center is a National Register district featuring 1880s Arts‑and‑Crafts buildings like Infinity Hall, an opera house with storefronts below.
The 1763 Blackberry River Inn, still standing, is listed on the National Register and dates back to colonial times.
Every summer, the Yale School of Music hosts the Norfolk Chamber Music Festival in the Music Shed on Ellen Battell Stoeckel’s estate.
The Norfolk Historical Society Museum offers a walking tour that lets visitors see how the town’s architecture evolved from 18th‑century maps to today.
